content.full_text:
May 2, 1977

[handwritten: 19 77-C 5-C43]

Robert Hilton
Attorney-at-Law
Room 6082
600 Grant Street
Pittsburgh, P.A. 15230

Dear Mr. Hilton:

This letter is in response to your telephone inquiry concerning
possible filing obligations under the Political Reform Act of
California for your client U.S. Steel.

You have stated that U.S. Steel has formed a Political Action
Committee which makes contributions to federal candidates and
various state candidates throughout the country. You have asked
what must be reported under California law when U.S. Steel
begins making contributions to California candidates.

In response to this inquiry I am sending you a copy of a letter
dated September 15, 1976 to Mr. Thomas I. McKnew, Jr., who is
the assistant general attorney for the Atchinson Topeka and
Santa Fe Railway Company. Mr. McKnew's question to the Commission
is very similar to yours and I believe that the response contained
in the letter will be an assistance to you.

If you have any additional questions after reading the McKnew
letter please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
  Coordinator


RSK:bw

Enclosure

REFERENCE:

Letter to Thomas
McKnew, AT&S,9/15/76

sections.question: What must be reported under California law when U.S. Steel begins making contributions to California candidates?
sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations under California's Political Reform Act when a corporation's Political Action Committee makes contributions to California candidates?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC directs the inquirer to a prior advisory letter to a similar inquiry from Atchinson, Topeka and Santa Fe Railway Company, dated September 15, 1976, which addresses comparable reporting requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains duplicate text (letter appears twice). No explicit CONCLUSION section; conclusion inferred from the FPPC's responsive guidance. Question is clearly stated in the body of the letter. Letter references an enclosure (the prior McKnew letter) but that enclosure is not included in the provided text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What must be reported under California law when U.S. Steel begins making contributions to California candidates?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC directs the inquirer to a prior advisory letter to a similar inquiry from Atchinson, Topeka and Santa Fe Railway Company, dated September 15, 1976, which addresses comparable reporting requirements.

embedding.summary: An FPPC response to Robert Hilton regarding U.S. Steel's reporting obligations under California's Political Reform Act when its Political Action Committee contributes to California candidates. The FPPC references a prior advisory letter addressing a similar inquiry rather than providing independent analysis.
